Infotexts.ntg.description.human_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Human Eye. Everyone starts with these!
Infotexts.ntg.causes.human_eye_damage: [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (Current strength times 0.05 per second) | [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.1 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.4 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.1 per second) | Being in Pressurized Water without protection (3% per tick) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.human_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.human_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.1 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.enhanced_eye_damage: Damage sustained to an Enhanced Human Eye.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.enhanced_eye_damage: [hypoxemia] Hypoxemia (Current strength times 0.004 per second) | [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.08 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.3 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.82 per second) | Being in Pressurized Water without protection (2.5% per tick) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.enhanced_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.enhanced_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.1 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.cyber_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Cybereye. These are immune to pressure and take no damage from afflictions on the body; though this also means they will not regenerate like a biological eye would.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.cyber_eye_damage: Vision impairment relative to the amount of damage sustained
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.cyber_eye_damage: [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [cyber_eye_repair_surgery] Cyber Eye Repair Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.plastic_eye_damage: The cheap and awful brother of the Cyber Eye. \nDamaged sustained to a Plastic Eye. These are immune to pressure and take no damage from afflictions on the body; though this also means they will not regenerate like a biological eye would.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.plastic_eye_damage: Vision impairment relative to the amount of damage sustained
Infotexts.ntg.description.husk_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Husk Eye. Though it is immune to pressure and lack of oxygen, it can still be damaged. Regenerates mildly faster.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.husk_eye_damage: [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.25 per second) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.husk_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.husk_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.2 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.crawler_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Crawler Eye. Though it is immune to pressure and lack of oxygen, it can still be damaged.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.crawler_eye_damage: [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.25 per second) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.crawler_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.crawler_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.1 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.mudraptor_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Mudraptor Eye. Though it is immune to pressure and lack of oxygen, it can still be damaged.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.mudraptor_eye_damage: [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.25 per second) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.mudraptor_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.mudraptor_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.1 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.hammerhead_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Hammerhead Eye. Though it is immune to pressure and lack of oxygen, it can still be damaged.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.hammerhead_eye_damage: [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.25 per second) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.hammerhead_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.hammerhead_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.1 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.watcher_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Watcher Eye. Though it is immune to pressure and lack of oxygen, it can still be damaged.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.watcher_eye_damage: [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.25 per second) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.watcher_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.watcher_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.1 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.latcher_eye_damage: Damage sustained to a Latcher Eye. Though it is immune to pressure and lack of oxygen, it can still be damaged.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.latcher_eye_damage: [stroke] Stroke (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [sepsis] Sepsis (Current strength times 0.03 per second) | [retinopathy] Retinopathy (Current strength times 0.25 per second) | Physically being damaged
Infotexts.ntg.effects.latcher_eye_damage: [cataract] May cause Cataracts (if over 60% eye damage) | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes (If both eyes recieve too much damage) | [dead_eye] Dead Eye (If only one eye recieves too much damage)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.latcher_eye_damage: Passive Regeneration (-0.1 damage per tick) | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [laser_eye_surgery] Laser Eye Surgery | [eye_drop_application] Eye Drop Application
Infotexts.ntg.description.dead_eyes: The result of recieving too much eye damage to both eyes. At this point, the eyes cannot be salvaged anymore and will not regenerate.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.dead_eyes: Eye damage, no matter your eye type (unless they are mechanical)
Infotexts.ntg.description.dead_eye: The result of recieving too much eye damage to a singular eye. At this point, the eye cannot be salvaged anymore and will not regenerate.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.dead_eye: Eye damage, no matter your eye type (unless they are synthetic)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.dead_eye: Partial blindness | [retinopathy] May cause Retinopathy in the remaining eye
Infotexts.ntg.description.retinopathy: Autoimmune Retinopathy, or damage to the retina, may be caused by having a singular dead eye if your immunity is above 10%. The other eye (if biological!) will rapidly sustain damage as the body overreacts. \nWill not be visible on the Health Scanner until it is above 5% strength; though symptoms will appear before then. \nThe possibility of this happening can be disabled in the config.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.retinopathy: Large amounts of eye damage, no matter your eye type (unless they are non-biological!)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.retinopathy: [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery to remove the dead eye | [azathioprine] Azathioprine (or other medication similar to it) that reduces your Immunity
Infotexts.ntg.description.cataract: Cataracts, or clouding of the lens of an eye, are the result of high levels of Eye Damage, though not enough to completely kill the eye. It can only happen in biological eyes. \nWill not be visible on the Health Scanner until it is above 15% strength; though symptoms will appear before then.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.cataract: Eye Damage, no matter your eye type (unless synthetic)
Infotexts.ntg.description.vision_sickness: Vision Sickness will occur after recieving new eyes, as the body (and your mind!) needs to adapt to them.
Infotexts.ntg.description.mismatch: A mismatch will occur if there are two different types of eye implanted; the brain has trouble keeping up and will have troubles with other tasks due to this.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.mismatch: Having a set of different eyes (For example, a Human Eye and a Cyber Eye at the same time)
Infotexts.ntg.treatments.mismatch: [corrective_glasses] Corrective Glasses will counteract the symptoms
Infotexts.ntg.description.removed_eyes: Either the result of surgical removal of the eyes, or the destruction of synthetic eyes.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.removed_eyes: [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | Too much Synthetic Eye damage
Infotexts.ntg.description.removed_eye: Either the result of surgical removal of an eye, or the destruction of a synthetic eye.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.removed_eye: [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | Too much Synthetic Eye damage
Infotexts.ntg.description.held_eyelids: The second step of all eye surgeries, where the eyelids are forced open to access the eye properly.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.held_eyelids: [skin_retractors] Using Skin Retractors on the head (Medical Skill Required: 25. Will cause between 0% to 7% Bleeding and 0% to 3% Eye Damage if failed)
Infotexts.ntg.description.popped_eyes: The third step of Eye Removal Surgery or Lens Application Surgery. The eyes are popped out to allow better access, though at the cost of patient visibility.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.popped_eyes: [tweezers] Using Tweezers on the head after Skin Retractors (Medical Skill Required: 30. Will cause between 0% to 10% Bleeding and 0% to 5% Eye Damage if failed)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.popped_eyes: Blurry vision and skill loss, scaling with affliction strength | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock if not near a Surgery Table
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.popped_eyes: [held_eyelids] Held Eyelids | [surgery_table] Surgery Table | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery | [lens_application_surgery] Lens Application Surgery | [bleeding] Bleeding
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_connector: The third step of Eye Transplant Surgery. Specialised eye connectors are applied to the eye socket in preparation for a new eye.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.eye_connector: [eye_connector_item] Using an Eye Connector on the head after Skin Retractors
Infotexts.ntg.description.cornea_incision: The third step of Cataract Surgery; capable of healing on its own if left alone. An incision is made into the cornea, allowing for replacement of the lens within the biological eye.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.cornea_incision: [scalpel] Using a Scalpel on the head after Skin Retractors | [multipurpose_scalpel] Using a Multipurpose Scalpel in Surgical Incision Mode on the head after Skin Retractors (Medical Skill Required: 45. Will cause between 0% to 25% Bleeding and 0% to 15 Eye% Damage if failed)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.cornea_incision: Blurry vision and skill loss, scaling with affliction strength | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock if done without sedation
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.cornea_incision: [cataract_surgery] Cataract Surgery | [organic_eye_lens] Organic Eye Lens | [analgesia] Analgesia
Infotexts.ntg.description.emulsification: The fourth step of Cataract Surgery. Will never heal on its own, requires the application of a new lens. The original cloudy lens is broken up into pieces and sucked out of the eye in order to be replaced with a new one.
Infotexts.ntg.causes.emulsification: [needle] Using a needle on the head after a Cornea Incision (Medical Skill Required: 50. Will cause between 0% to 7% Eye Damage if failed)
Infotexts.ntg.effects.emulsification: Blurry vision and skill loss, scaling with affliction strength | [traumatic_shock] Traumatic Shock if not near a Surgery Table
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.emulsification: [cataract_surgery] Cataract Surgery | [cornea_incision] Cornea Incision | [surgery_table] Surgery Table
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_drops: The result of application of specialised eye drops to the eyes, enhancing natural regeneration.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.eye_drops: Increases natural eye healing speed by 4x for the duration of the affliction (though only on biological eyes!)
Infotexts.ntg.description.laser_surgery: The result of skill application of a Laser Surgery Tool, supercharging natural regeneration of the eyes.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.laser_surgery: Increases natural eye healing speed by 16x for the duration of the affliction (though only on biological eyes!)
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_removal_surgery: Medical Skill Check: 45. \nSurgery to remove one or both eyes - alive, dead or synthetic - in order to replace them, prevent extra damage or to use them in an Eye Regeneration Jar. If the eye(s) is/are still alive, they will be put into the inventory of the one performing the surgery; in case their inventory is full, they will be dropped on the ground! \nShould be done near a Surgery Table.
Infotexts.ntg.steps.eye_removal_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Apply Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Apply Anesthesia | [skin_retractors] 2. Apply Skin Retractors to cause Held Eyelids | [tweezers] 3. Apply Tweezers to cause Popped Eyes | [multipurpose_scalpel] 4a. Apply a Multipurpose Scalpel in the Surgical Incision mode | [scalpel] 4b. Apply a Scalpel
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.eye_removal_surgery: [dead_eye] Dead Eye | [dead_eyes] Dead Eyes | [eye_transplant_surgery] Eye Transplant Surgery | [popped_eyes] Popped Eyes | [held_eyelids] Held Eyelids |
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_transplant_surgery: Medical Skill Check: 45. \nSurgery to grant the patient a new eye. Only works if there is not already an eye - alive, dead or synthetic - in the eye socket. Remove them first!
Infotexts.ntg.steps.eye_transplant_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Apply Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Apply Anesthesia | [skin_retractors] 2. Apply Skin Retractors to cause Held Eyelids | [eye_connector_item] 3. Apply Eye Connector | 4. Insert an eye of your choice
Infotexts.ntg.description.cataract_surgery: Medical Skill Check: 50. \nSurgery to remove a Cataract; ensure you have an Organic Eye Lens ready to go!
Infotexts.ntg.steps.cataract_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Apply Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Apply Anesthesia | [skin_retractors] 2. Apply Skin Retractors to cause Held Eyelids | [multipurpose_scalpel] 3a. Apply a Multipurpose Scalpel in the Surgical Incision mode to cause a Cornea Incision | [scalpel] 3b. Apply a Scalpel to cause a Cornea Incision | [needle] 4. Apply a Needle to cause Emulsification | [organic_eye_lens] 5. Apply an Organic Eye Lens to remove the Cataract
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.cataract_surgery: [held_eyelids] Held Eyelids | [cornea_incision] Cornea Incision | [emulsification] Emulsification | [eye_removal_surgery] Eye Removal Surgery
Infotexts.ntg.description.cyber_eye_repair_surgery: Medical Skill Check: 30. \nSurgery to repair Cyber Eyes.
Infotexts.ntg.steps.cyber_eye_repair_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Apply Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Apply Anesthesia | [skin_retractors] 2. Apply Skin Retractors to cause Held Eyelids | [tweezers] 3. Apply Tweezers to cause Popped Eyes | 4. Apply an FPGA Circuit (repairs 20% Damage per application)
Infotexts.ntg.description.lens_application_surgery: Medical Skill Check: 30. \nA surgery to apply a specialised lens into an eye capable of holding it, allowing for a brand new perspective.
Infotexts.ntg.steps.lens_application_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Apply Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Apply Anesthesia | [skin_retractors] 2. Apply Skin Retractors to cause Held Eyelids | [tweezers] 3. Apply Tweezers to cause Popped Eyes | 4. Apply a lens of your choice
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.lens_application_surgery: [medical_lens] Medical Lens | [electrical_lens] Electrical Lens | [night_vision_lens] Night Vision Lens | [thermal_lens] Thermal Lens | [enhanced_eye] Enhanced Eye | [cyber_eye] Cyber Eye
Infotexts.ntg.description.lens_removal_surgery: Medical Skill Check: 40. \nA surgery to remove a specialised lens from within the eye. You could also just remove the entire eye.
Infotexts.ntg.steps.lens_removal_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Apply Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Apply Anesthesia | [skin_retractors] 2. Apply Skin Retractors to cause Held Eyelids | [tweezers] 3. Apply Tweezers to cause Popped Eyes | 4. Apply a screwdriver
Infotexts.ntg.description.laser_eye_surgery: Medical Skill Check: 80. \nAn advanced surgery to supercharge natural eye healing. Only works on biological eyes.
Infotexts.ntg.steps.laser_eye_surgery: [analgesia] 1a. Apply Analgesia | [anesthesia] 1b. Apply Anesthesia | [skin_retractors] 2. Apply Skin Retractors to cause Held Eyelids | [laser_surgery_tool] 3. Apply a Laser Surgery Tool
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_drop_application: A procedure to enhance natural eye healing. Only works on biological eyes.
Infotexts.ntg.description.human_eye: Just a regular human eye - everyone has a couple. Probably.
Infotexts.ntg.description.enhanced_eye: A regular human eye, enhanced with electrical circuits and plastic.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.enhanced_eye: Allows you to see | [lens_application_surgery] Allows cyber lenses to be installed using Lens Application Surgery | Better damage resistance
Infotexts.ntg.description.cyber_eye: Costly synthetic eyes. Can't be damaged by bodily afflictions, can't be naturally healed. Resistant to watter pressure and does not need to be refrigerated.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.cyber_eye: Allows you to see better | [lens_application_surgery] Allows cyber lenses to be installed using Lens Application Surgery | [cyber_repair_surgery] Must be repaired using Cyber Repair Surgery | High damage resistance
Infotexts.ntg.description.plastic_eye: Pseudo eyes for those too poor to afford real ones; though they are resistant to water pressure and do not need to be refrigerated. Will melt if exposed to fire, though. Will not heal naturally nor be damaged by bodily afflictions; cannot be repaired!
Infotexts.ntg.effects.plastic_eye: Plastic Vision (applies a unique overlay on your screen)
Infotexts.ntg.description.husk_eye: Pressure-resistant eyes extracted from a Husk; will distort your vision - but at least they will not infect you!
Infotexts.ntg.description.crawler_eye: Pressure-resistant eyes extracted from a Crawler; better suited for low-light environments, though at a mild cost.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.crawler_eye: Reduced skills | Better vision in the dark
Infotexts.ntg.description.mudraptor_eye: Pressure-resistant eyes extracted from a Mudraptor; better suited for low-light environments, though at a mild cost. Looks like Europa herself.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.mudraptor_eye: Reduced skills | Better vision in the dark
Infotexts.ntg.description.hammerhead_eye: Pressure- and damage resistant eyes extracted from a Hammerhead; better suited for low-light environments, though at a mild cost.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.hammerhead_eye: Reduced skills | Better vision in the dark | Better damage resistance
Infotexts.ntg.description.watcher_eye: Pressure-resistant eyes extracted from a Watcher; better suited for low-light environments and with its excellent vision increases your aptitude for tasks. May cause the mind of yourself and your crew to struggle, however.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.watcher_eye: Increased skills (25% more) | Better vision in the dark | May cause Psychosis
Infotexts.ntg.description.latcher_eye: Pressure-resistant eyes extracted from a Latcher; better suited for low-light environments and allow you to 'sense' life-forms through walls.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.latcher_eye: Better vision in the dark | Better damage resistance | See life-forms through walls, vaguely
Infotexts.ntg.description.medical_lens:
An advanced cyberlens that's used during Lens Application Surgery to upgrade Enhanced / Cyber Eyes. 
Gives 10 Medical Skill and enables Health Scanner functionality when installed: 
- Press F to scan the selected limb while in the health UI; 
- Press F outside of the health UI to turn the ranged version on/off, allowing you to see afflictions from a distance.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.medical_lens: [lens_application_surgery] Lens Application Surgery | [cyber_eye] Cyber Eye | [enhanced_eye] Enhanced Eye
Infotexts.ntg.description.electrical_lens: An advanced cyberlens that's used during Lens Application Surgery to upgrade Enhanced / Cyber Eyes. \nGives 15 Electrical Skill and enables Electrical Vision. Press F to turn it on/off.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.electrical_lens: [lens_application_surgery] Lens Application Surgery | [cyber_eye] Cyber Eye | [enhanced_eye] Enhanced Eye
Infotexts.ntg.description.night_vision_lens: An advanced cyberlens that's used during Lens Application Surgery to upgrade Enhanced / Cyber Eyes. \nEnables Night Vision. Cannot be turned off.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.night_vision_lens: [lens_application_surgery] Lens Application Surgery | [cyber_eye] Cyber Eye | [enhanced_eye] Enhanced Eye
Infotexts.ntg.description.thermal_lens: An advanced cyberlens that's used during Lens Application Surgery to upgrade Enhanced / Cyber Eyes. \nGives 15 Weapons Skill and enables Thermal Vision. Press F to turn it on/off.
Infotexts.ntg.seealso.thermal_lens: [lens_application_surgery] Lens Application Surgery | [cyber_eye] Cyber Eye | [enhanced_eye] Enhanced Eye
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_connector_item: Used in Eye Transplant Surgery. Each application can be used to transplant 1 eye, and it holds 4 applications.
Infotexts.ntg.description.organic_eye_lens: A replacement lens to use in in Cataract Surgery.
Infotexts.ntg.description.laser_surgery_tool: Medical Skill Check: 80. \nAn advanced handheld surgery tool to enhance the healing process. Will cause major eye damage if not applied properly, and consumes a lot of power.
Infotexts.ntg.description.the_spoon: A highly advanced tool to allow for eyeball extraction on dead creatures; though this may damage them. \nIf you're trying to craft this, it's called 'The Spoon' in the fabricator.
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_drops_item: Medical Skill Check: None. \nUsed to make eyes heal faster by applying to the eyes. Does not work on Synthetic eyes.
Infotexts.ntg.description.corrective_glasses: A set of corrective glasses that help remedy the downsides of eye damage, cataracts or mismatching eyes. They do not cure the affliction, only counter the effects.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.corrective_glasses: [blurred_vision] Prevents the effects of Blurred Vision | [mismatch] Prevents the effects of Mismatch
Infotexts.ntg.description.night_vision_goggles: A battery powered set of goggles that enhance vision in the dark.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.night_vision_goggles: Allows you to see in the dark
Infotexts.ntg.description.eye_regeneration_jar: A jar filled with a special liquid that helps regenerate biological eyes. This does not work on synthetic eyes. It can be placed down and holds two eyes.
Infotexts.ntg.effects.eye_regeneration_jar: Heals the stored eye(s) slowly over time
